nodes.label: Optimal Placement of Reference Nodes for Wireless Indoor Positioning (Aomumpai et al., ECTI-CON 2014)
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. Optimal placement of reference nodes for wireless indoor positioning systems : 2014 11th In.pdf
nodes.author: Supattra Aomumpai, Kittipob Kondee, Chutima Prommak, Kamol Kaemarungsi

nodes.label: Binary Integer Linear Programming for Reference Node Placement
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. Optimal placement of reference nodes for wireless indoor positioning systems : 2014 11th In.pdf
nodes.rationale: Formulating reference node placement as BILP minimizes the number of nodes while maximizing coverage, yielding up to 5 meters accuracy improvement at 90th percentile over ad-hoc deployments.

nodes.label: Offline/Online Phases of RSS Fingerprinting-Based Localization
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. Optimal placement of reference nodes for wireless indoor positioning systems : 2014 11th In.pdf
nodes.rationale: The two-phase structure (offline survey + online matching) is universal to fingerprinting systems; offline builds a radio map by RSS site survey, online matches live RSS to the map. This design separates calibration cost from operational cost.
